reasoning: The DJIA value on October 24, 2025 reached a high of 47,207.12, culminating a nearly 500-point single-day surge that placed it significantly above its short-term linear trendline (which projects to roughly 46,770 by Nov 2). Due to this strong deviation from trend, short-term mean reversion makes it less likely (around 35%) that the index remains above this exact peak by Nov 2. As the forecast horizon extends to November 25 and January 24, the natural upward drift of the stock market and favorable Q4 seasonal tailwinds begin to counteract this initial mean-reversion pressure. Consequently, the probabilities trend back toward 0.5 as uncertainty increases with the longer time horizons, strictly adhering to the mandated requirement that probabilities for more distant dates regress closer to 50%.
batch_queries: Will the Dow Jones Industrial Average have increased by each resolution date listed below as compared to its value on 2025-10-26?
